label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read this letter from Carmen Rivera carefully. The main point is that she's writing to Disney after a visit to the Magic Kingdom. She mentions an event that made their visit seem "truly magical". The event involves their daughter Gloria and an ice-cream. They couldn't take the ice-cream on Space Mountain, so a staff member named Murphy offered to hold it and later gave Gloria a new one after the ride. Carmen explains that Murphy knew the ice-cream wouldn't last, so she bought a new one right before they exited. The letter ends with Carmen thanking Disney for going "above and beyond". Now, the question is asking what Carmen wrote the letter for. The options are (A) to order ice-creams, (B) to promise to visit again, (C) to express her thanks, or (D) to give some suggestions. Looking at the letter's content and the closing sentence, the main purpose is clearly to thank Disney for the staff member's kind action. The
